title: Accelerated remitter notification and questionnaire - T216 - Canada.ca
content:
Remit (pay) payroll deductions and contributions

## Accelerated remitter notification and questionnaire - T216
- When is the T216 sent
- What methods the CRA uses to send you the T216
- When do you need to respond to the T216
- How to respond to the T216
The CRA may send you a T216 - Accelerated remitter notification and questionnaire if you are a regular or quarterly remitter and the average monthly withholding amount (AMWA) was at least $25,000 in the previous year .
The T216 determines if the remitter type for your payroll accounts will change to an accelerated remitter for the next calendar year based on the AMWA of either :
- Your single payroll account
- All of your multiple or associated corporations payroll accounts
An employer is a regular remitter who had an AMWA of $30,000 in 2023 . The employer will receive a T216 in July 2024 . The response to that T216 will determine if the employer will be an accelerated remitter in 2025 .
Learn more about the different types of remitters: Types of remitters .

## When is the T216 sent
The accelerated remitter notification and questionnaire (T216) is sent in June .

## What methods the CRA uses to send you the T216
The T216 will be sent to you in the following methods:
- Electronically when you are registered for My Business Account (MyBA) or Represent a Client (RAC) using the online services at CRA sign-in services .
- Mail whether or not you are registered for the electronic services

## When do you need to respond to the T216
You have to respond to the T216 by the last Friday of July .
Once the CRA has reviewed your response, if your remitter type changes, a notice will be sent in November on when and how to remit (pay) in the following year .

### What if you do not respond to the T216
If you do not respond to the T216, a notice will be sent to inform you that the remitter type for your payroll account was changed to an accelerated remitter.

## How to respond to the T216
You can respond by sending your completed T216 using one of the following methods:
- My Business Account (MyBA) or Represent a Client (RAC) using the online services at CRA sign-in services
- By mail using the return mail address on the form
- By fax to your National Verification and Collection Centre: Newfoundland and Labrador 1-418-562-4205 Toll-free: 1-833-675-9018 Shawinigan 1-418-562-4193 Toll-free: 1-833-697-2399 Surrey 1-418-562-4060 Toll-free: 1-888-226-2081 Winnipeg 1-418-562-4197 Toll-free: 1-833-697-2400
By fax to your National Verification and Collection Centre:
- Toll-free: 1-833-675-9018
- Toll-free: 1-833-697-2399
- Toll-free: 1-888-226-2081
- Toll-free: 1-833-697-2400

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
